note: Pakistani applicants: any German student visa needs an APS certificate — it can take 2-3 months. Start it now.
Non-EU tuition at public universities is about €727 per semester. Many programs also ask for proof you would qualify for the same program in Pakistan — check each university.

Public tuition is €1,000–4,000 per year and drops sharply with the income-based ISEE calculation — low-income students can pay close to zero and may also win a DSU regional grant. Pre-enrolment runs through Universitaly, and degree verification (CIMEA / Declaration of Value) takes time — start early.

Non-EU tuition at public universities is about €3,900 per year for a Master's, and many universities waive part or all of it. Pakistani applicants MUST go through Campus France's 'Études en France' procedure — its window closes months before university deadlines, often December–February for autumn intake.
Study in Czech is free, but English-taught programs charge tuition. Your Pakistani degree usually needs recognition (nostrification) — start early.
Public universities charge non-EU students in English programs (often €1,000–2,500 per semester). Deadlines for October intake often fall in June–July — check fast.
Self-funded tuition is significant here — most Pakistani students fund Hungary through the Stipendium Hungaricum scholarship (see the Scholarships tab).
Public does not mean cheap here: non-EU tuition for CS Master's is usually €15,000–22,000 per year. Apply via Studielink plus each university's own portal; non-EU deadlines often fall January–April.

Non-EU tuition is roughly €12,000–15,000 per year, but there is ONE national portal for every university and one main round: opens around mid-October, closes around mid-January, with a SEK 900 application fee. Swedish Institute and university scholarships can cover costs — see the Scholarships tab.

Non-EU tuition is €10,000–18,000 per year, BUT most universities grant big automatic scholarships (50–100% tuition waivers) to strong admitted applicants. One national application service; the main joint window is usually in January.

Norway ended free tuition for non-EU students in 2023 — expect five-figure yearly tuition that varies a lot by program. Old blogs still say 'free'; they are wrong. Salaried PhD positions remain an exception.

Public universities are very cheap for international students, with strong English-taught CS programs. Each university runs its own international application, usually between May and August. Türkiye Bursları (Scholarships tab) is the fully funded route.
A different model: admission to graduate programs at KAUST or KFUPM comes WITH full funding — tuition, monthly stipend, and housing. Admission and scholarship are one application.

Public universities charge international Master's students roughly €1,500–3,500 per year in total. Apply directly to each university; there are usually two intakes (around March and around September–October). EMGS processes the student visa after admission.

Self-funded English-taught CS Master's at public universities typically cost €2,500–5,000 per year. Applications go to each university's international portal, mostly December–May. Most Pakistani students pair admission with the CSC scholarship — see the Scholarships tab.
